Sunday.name: Solar Plexus Chakra - Manipura
Sunday.visualization: Gentle rays of the sun, a rich yellow wheat field
Sunday.roleAndPurpose: The third chakra is the most complex and is also known as the mental power center. Its role includes responsibility for our opinions, beliefs, and the ways we judge — mental patterns we have adopted since childhood. It strongly influences self-esteem and self-confidence. This is the cognitive center, knowing how to function in everyday frameworks. Another important role of this energy center is to act as an "energy pump" for the physical body and the other subtle bodies.
commonIssues: Liver, spleen, stomach, bladder, pancreas, and kidney problems
guidedReflections: Do I trust myself to make the right decisions, or do I constantly seek external approval?
Where in my life do I give away my power, and why do I allow it?
Do I let fear of failure stop me from taking bold action toward my goals?
How do I handle criticism — does it crush my confidence or fuel my growth?
What limiting beliefs about my worth or capabilities did I inherit from family or society?
When was the last time I stood up for myself without guilt or apology?
Do I take full responsibility for my choices, or do I tend to blame external circumstances?
How do I balance humility with pride in my accomplishments?
What would my life look like if I fully trusted my inner fire — my intuition, will, and confidence?
Sunday.inquiry: Where am I ready to step up or expand?

Monday.name: Sacral Chakra - Svadhisthana
Monday.bodyAreas: Ovaries, testes, prostate, sexual organs, spleen, uterus, urinary bladder
Monday.visualization: Sunrise, swimming in pure natural waters on full moon
Monday.roleAndPurpose: The second chakra is the center of our emotions and creativity. Through it, we begin to understand our responses to both our internal and external worlds. From here, we create through emotion and express it creatively to the outside world through art or communication, as well as develop understanding and empathy for the feelings of others around us. In this energy center, we start to grasp the value of relationships and life partnerships, the need to express emotions, and to feel the other.
commonIssues: Menstrual cycle problems and ovarian issues
guidedReflections: Do I allow myself to fully feel my emotions, or do I suppress them to appear in control?
In what areas of life do I resist flow and spontaneity, and why?
How often do I create for the joy of creating, rather than for validation or outcome?
Do I connect with others from genuine emotional openness or from a place of need and attachment?
How do I express my sexuality — as an act of love, power, connection, or escape?
Do I feel shame or guilt around my desires, and where did that conditioning come from?
How do I balance giving and receiving affection, attention, and energy in my relationships?
Do I allow myself to cry, laugh, and express emotion freely, or do I censor my feelings to please others?
What would it feel like to live each day as a creative, sensual, emotionally honest being?
Monday.inquiry: What am I feeling right now without judging it?

Tuesday.name: Root Chakra - Muladhara
Tuesday.bodyAreas: Kidneys, spine, large intestine, legs, bones
Tuesday.location: At the base of the spine, in the space between the genitals and the anus.
Tuesday.visualization: Circulating blood, flowing lava at the center of the earth
Tuesday.roleAndPurpose: The role of the first chakra is the survival instinct in the physical world. It is connected to our primal emotions and programmed by the family, friends, and society (our tribe) in which we live. It operates in the present moment — nothing happens until one of our five senses receives specific information and we experience or touch it. Throughout life, the root chakra regulates our physical desires and needs such as shelter, money, food, drink, relationships, and the desire to reproduce and continue our lineage. This is the source of the basic programming of what we believe we deserve in life and our desire to live it. In this energy center lie our roots, family values, beliefs, and heritage. Here is the source of our feelings toward ourselves and our right to exist and take up space in the physical world, as well as our right to be loved.
commonIssues: Source of addictions and uncontrollable desires
Poor blood circulation and skin issues
Reproductive system problems, as well as headaches
Issues related to money, career, and financial matters
guidedReflections: When I think of the word security, what does it truly mean to me beyond money or possessions?
Do I feel safe existing as I am, without needing to prove or justify my worth?
How much of my current belief system about success, survival, or family comes directly from my upbringing rather than from my own truth?
Where in my life do I still operate out of fear of not having enough — enough love, money, stability, or belonging?
In what areas of my life do I struggle with boundaries or the right to occupy space?
How do I relate to the concept of having — do I allow myself to receive, or do I unconsciously push abundance away?
What are my coping mechanisms when I feel unsafe or unsupported, and how do they affect my health or habits?
Do I trust life to provide for me, or do I feel I must constantly fight for survival?
What would my life look like if I felt fully grounded, secure, and connected to the Earth at all times?
Tuesday.inquiry: What concrete action strengthens my life today?

Wednesday.name: Throat Chakra - Vishuddha
Wednesday.bodyAreas: Throat, bronchi, voice mechanism, lungs, digestive tract, mouth
Wednesday.visualization: Blue clear skies, sky reflections on calm water
Wednesday.roleAndPurpose: The fifth chakra is our communication center and the foundation for creating our future and self-protection. Through it, we express our thoughts, feelings, experiences, what we want, need, and also what we do not want. It is an important energy center that gives us the power to say "yes" or "no" to options life offers, thus bringing personal responsibility for ourselves and our environment. Through it, we broadcast to the outside world what is happening in our inner world. It is based on speaking truth and on accepting and giving help and guidance.
commonIssues: Problems with throat, jaw, mouth, tonsils, and pharynx
fundamentalTruths: I feel, think, and express with love!
Surrender your will to the divine will!
guidedReflections: Do I express my truth openly, or do I censor myself to avoid conflict or judgment?
How often do I truly listen — not just hear — what others are saying?
In what situations do I find it hardest to say "no," and what fears keep me from setting that boundary?
Do my words reflect my heart's truth, or are they shaped to please, control, or protect?
What unspoken truths have been living in my throat, waiting to be released?
Do I trust that my voice has power, and that what I say matters?
How do I nourish myself with words — the ones I speak to myself and others?
What is my relationship with silence — do I find peace in it or discomfort?
Do I communicate my needs clearly, or do I expect others to sense or guess them?
Wednesday.inquiry: What truth wants to be spoken or written?

Thursday.name: Third Eye Chakra - Ajna
Thursday.symbol: Lotus with 96 petals (2 lotuses with 48 petals each)
Thursday.bodyAreas: Lower brain, left eye, ears, nose, nervous system
Thursday.location: Center of the forehead, one finger above the eyebrows.
Thursday.visualization: Starry night skies, celestial bodies, galaxies
Thursday.roleAndPurpose: The sixth chakra, known since ancient times as the "third eye," is our center of vision — both inner and outer. Through it, we see reality, record experiences, and broadcast images, symbols, colors, and visions that represent our reality. It is closely connected to the pituitary gland, which regulates our hormones as part of the powerful endocrine control system, shaping the body's cellular health based on the personal image we visualize and our vision for higher goals. The sixth energy center is also responsible for our good mood and behavior through influence on hormones secreted in the brain.
fundamentalTruths: I feel, think, and express my vision with love!
guidedReflections: How clearly do I see my life direction right now?
Do I use my imagination creatively or allow it to be clouded by worry and fantasy?
How do I visualize my future, and does that vision align with who I truly am?
How connected do I feel to my inner wisdom and higher guidance?
When was the last time I had a powerful intuitive insight, and how did I respond to it?
In what ways do I nourish or neglect my mental clarity and vision?
Do I allow myself to dream and create visions that feel expansive and inspired, or do I tend to play small?
Do I trust my intuition as a valid source of truth, or do I dismiss it in favor of logic and reason?
How can I open my third eye further through daily practice — such as meditation, visualization, or mindful awareness of my inner images?
Thursday.inquiry: What does my intuition know that my mind doesn't?

Friday.name: Heart Chakra - Anahata
Friday.location: Center of the chest, heart area.
Friday.visualization: Green and blooming nature, plant blossoms, pink and endless skies
Friday.roleAndPurpose: The fourth chakra is our heart center and is considered the point of balance and middle. It is located above the three lower centers and below the three upper centers, at the body's center. Its role is to perfectly combine the lessons learned from the other chakras, to blend them with universal love and compassion, and to radiate this energy outward from a true, stable, and constant place. As humans, we aspire to live through the heart; this is where our success lies. One of the important lessons of this energy center is to accept love inwardly from the environment and give love outwardly from within. Another important role is healing — the ability to care for ourselves and others with love. "Our longest and most important journey in life is from the head to the heart."
commonIssues: Inability to give and receive love
Poor blood circulation, unstable blood pressure
fundamentalTruths: I feel and think with love!
guidedReflections: Do I allow myself to receive love as easily as I give it?
What old emotional wounds still close my heart, and what would it take to begin healing them?
Do I love conditionally, expecting something in return, or do I allow love to flow freely?
How easily do I forgive myself and others, and what resistance do I feel toward forgiveness?
How comfortable am I with vulnerability — expressing my true feelings without fear of rejection?
What does living "from the heart" look like in my daily life?
Do I honor my own emotional needs as much as I honor others'?
How can I cultivate more compassion toward myself, especially in moments of failure or weakness?
What would change in my world if I chose to let love, not fear, guide every decision?
Friday.inquiry: Where can I offer more compassion today - to myself or others?

Saturday.name: Crown Chakra - Sahasrara
Saturday.bodyAreas: Upper brain, right eye, cerebral cortex, central nervous system
Saturday.location: Top of the head, crown of the skull, the soft spot of a baby.
Saturday.visualization: Peak of a very high snowy mountain
Saturday.roleAndPurpose: The seventh chakra is our divinity center. It is the point that centralizes streams of high spiritual energy, such as guidance from above for our role and mission in this life, bringing it into the physical body through the pineal gland and cerebral cortex. It also charges our subtle energy bodies with high vibrations. The crown chakra regulates many functions inside and outside the body. Another important role of this energy center is to remind us that we are one with the source and all spiritual beings.
commonIssues: All problems related to the pineal gland
Problems related to a faulty immune system such as cancer or neurological disorders
fundamentalTruths: I feel, think, and express the vision of a higher purpose with love!
guidedReflections: How connected do I feel to a higher source, universal energy, or divine presence in my daily life?
How often do I live fully in the present moment, rather than dwelling on the past or future?
Do I trust that my life has a meaningful purpose, and can I sense what it is?
How do I integrate spiritual insight or guidance into my everyday actions?
In what ways do I honor my highest potential and express it in the world?
Do I perceive life as a series of separate events, or as an interconnected, unified flow?
How do I cultivate stillness and inner silence to access wisdom beyond the mind?
What practices help me feel aligned with the divine, and do I commit to them consistently?
How can I embody the awareness that I am not separate from the universe, but an integral part of it?
Saturday.inquiry: What is awareness itself noticing right now?
